name: Sierra de Guadarrama Towns Loop
name_original: La Navata, Moralzarzal, Navacerrada, Guadarrama – ruta circular
description: This moderate 36.3-mile road cycling route loops through La Navata, Moralzarzal, Navacerrada, and Guadarrama in the Sierra de Guadarrama.
region_intro: Road cycling around Galapagar offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain within the Community of Madrid. The region features mountainous areas, including proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, providing routes with significant elevation gain. Cyclists can also find paths around the scenic Valmayor Reservoir and through river basins, offering a mix of environments. These natural features contribute to a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Galapagar The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Guadarrama Towns Loop, a 36.3 miles (58.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Escorial Monastery and Cruz Verde Pass Loop, a difficult 55.9 miles (90.0 km) path. This route features significant climbs and passes by the historic El Escorial Monastery. Local road cyclists also love the Views of Valmayor Reservoir – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Galapagar - La Navata, a 41.3 miles (66.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Valmayor Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Galapagar is defined by mountainous areas, scenic reservoirs, and river basins. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. The routes in Galapagar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Galapagar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Galapagar?
A: Galapagar offers a diverse selection of road cycling routes. There are currently 18 routes listed, catering to various skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Galapagar?
A: Road cycling around Galapagar features a varied and engaging landscape. You'll encounter mountainous areas, particularly as you approach the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, offering challenging ascents and descents. Routes also wind around scenic features like the Valmayor Reservoir and through diverse river basins, providing a mix of environments from foothills to meadows. The region's natural beauty includes pine and oak forests, adding to the scenic appeal.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain in Galapagar?
A: Yes, Galapagar is an excellent area for cyclists seeking significant elevation gain, especially due to its proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977469162/el-escorial-monastery-and-cruz-verde-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Escorial Monastery and Cruz Verde Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 55.9 miles (90.0 km), feature substantial climbs, with an elevation gain of over 1,700 meters. This route passes by the historic El Escorial Monastery, combining a physical challenge with cultural interest.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I discover along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can enjoy scenic vistas of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a prominent highlight in the area. The mountainous terrain near the Sierra de Guadarrama provides panoramic views, and you might even spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2505177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Abantos&lt;/a&gt; summit. Some trails also lead through ecological areas rich in biodiversity, such as oak and ash groves.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. Galapagar's location allows cyclists to incorporate visits to significant historical and cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977469162/el-escorial-monastery-and-cruz-verde-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Escorial Monastery and Cruz Verde Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past the iconic Royal Monastery of El Escorial. While not directly on all routes, the Church of San Bartolomé (Hermitage of El Cerrillo) is a notable local landmark dating back to 1447 that can be explored.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Galapagar?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Galapagar are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the region. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977460304/sierra-de-guadarrama-towns-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Guadarrama Towns Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.3-mile (58.4 km) route that navigates through the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Another great circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31804618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Valmayor Reservoir – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Galapagar - La Navata&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and reservoir views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Galapagar?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the midday sun, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter can bring cooler temperatures, particularly in the mountainous areas, but many days remain suitable for cycling.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Galapagar?
A: While many routes in Galapagar feature moderate to difficult terrain, there are easier options suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. The guide includes 3 easy routes, which typically have less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, making them more accessible for a family outing. Always check the route's difficulty and distance to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Galapagar?
A: Galapagar and its surrounding towns offer various parking options. Many routes start from or pass through urban centers where public parking is available. For routes beginning outside town, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or points of interest. It's advisable to check specific route details for recommended starting points and parking facilities.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Galapagar using public transport?
A: Yes, Galapagar is part of the Community of Madrid's transport network, making it accessible by public transport. Regional buses connect Galapagar with Madrid and other nearby towns. Some train lines also serve the broader area, potentially allowing access to starting points for certain routes. Cyclists should verify public transport options and bike carriage policies with the respective operators before planning their trip.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Galapagar?
A: The road cycling experience in Galapagar is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain climbs to scenic reservoir views. The variety of routes, catering to different skill levels, and the opportunity to combine cycling with visits to historical landmarks like El Escorial Monastery are also frequently highlighted as major attractions.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural monuments along the routes?
A: While the region is known for its reservoirs and mountainous terrain, you can find specific natural monuments. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature in the area. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6277005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone slab above the Valmayor reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers a unique geological point of interest.
tips: Probably its star monument due to its strange and unusual existence is the Entreterminos dolmen, an authentic megalith dating from around the 4th millennium BC. c.
It maintains a careful historic center. The most notable monuments are the church of San Miguel Arcángel, the Plaza de la Constitución, the town hall and the modern hermitage of Santa Cruz.

Guadarrama gives its name to the homonymous mountain range. It is located in the foothills and is a crossroads for cycling routes. From here you can climb the ports of León, Navacerrada or Cruz Verde, towards San Lorenzo de El Escorial.

faqs.question: How difficult is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as a moderate road ride. It covers 58.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of 700 meters, including climbs typical of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain passes. A good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete this 58.4-kilometer route in approximately 2 hours and 43 minutes, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You'll encounter ascents through mountain passes and descents, with varied scenery from open landscapes to sections with thick forest.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guadarrama region is generally best for cycling in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder conditions and potential ice at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any notable towns or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects several towns, including La Navata, Moralzarzal, Navacerrada, and Guadarrama. You'll pass through areas like Moralzarzal Town Center and the Main Square of Guadarrama, offering opportunities for breaks and to experience local life. The broader region offers picturesque mountain views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular route?
faqs.answer: You can find parking in the town centers along the route, such as La Navata, Moralzarzal, Navacerrada, or Guadarrama. For example, parking is available near the La Navata train station or in the centers of Moralzarzal and Guadarrama.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting points of this route using public transport?
faqs.answer: The towns along the route, including La Navata, Moralzarzal, Navacerrada, and Guadarrama, are accessible by public transport from Madrid, typically by bus or train. Check local transport schedules for the most convenient options to your preferred starting point.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in the Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits or entrance fees are required for road cycling on public roads within the Sierra de Guadarrama. However, always respect local regulations and protected areas. For specific details, it's advisable to check the official tourism website for the region, such as visitmadrid.es, for any updates.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, primarily designed for bicycles. While dogs are generally welcome in the Sierra de Guadarrama, cycling with a dog on a road bike can be challenging and potentially unsafe for both the cyclist and the dog. It's not typically recommended for this type of activity.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with or run alongside several other paths. Notable intersecting trails include parts of the Ruta ADESGAM, the Camino de Santiago, and the GR 10.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or resupply along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. As the route passes through several towns like Moralzarzal, Navacerrada, and Guadarrama, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel, grab a meal, or purchase supplies.
